notes.anchor: I am excused before Appuleius day by day
notes.note: Appuleius was the augur presiding over the augural college; Cicero, a member, needed a formal excuse renewed daily to be absent from its meetings while he grieved at Astura. The "standing excuse" (a single blanket leave) had been refused, so the absence had to be entered day by day.

notes.anchor: all my talk is with books
notes.note: Cicero's reading and writing at Astura in this period fed the philosophical works of grief, above all the lost Consolatio he composed for his daughter Tullia.
